etymology.description: semantic 女 (female, woman) + phonetic 未 -> younger sister
etymology.interpretation: The character 妹 (mèi, "younger sister") uses 女 (nǚ, "female, woman") for its semantic part, indicating the gender, while 未 (wèi) provides the pronunciation. This combination suggests a female family member distinct from an older sister, leading to the meaning "younger sister."

examples.chinese: 我有一个妹妹(我有一個妹妹)。
妹妹喜欢画画(妹妹喜歡畫畫)，她常常在家里画风景(她常常在家裡畫風景)。
examples.pinyin: Mèimei xǐhuān huàhuà, tā chángcháng zài jiālǐ huà fēngjǐng.
examples.english: My younger sister likes painting, she often paints landscapes at home.

examples.chinese: 我送给妹妹一个新书包(我送給妹妹一個新書包)，她很开心(她很開心)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ sòng gěi mèimei yī gè xīn shūbāo, tā hěn kāixīn.
examples.english: I gave my younger sister a new backpack, she was very happy.
